name: Bavarian
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Bavarian is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Bavarian (Boarisch) is an Upper German variety of Bavaria, Austria and South Tyrol, written in the Latin script. It has NO single official orthography: writers use German spelling conventions adapted ad hoc, and the phoneme–grapheme mapping differs substantially between the Northern, Central and Southern dialect areas, so a single sourced letter→IPA table cannot honestly be given. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script (German-convention) Bavarian text. See also the existing de-x-bavarian spec for the Bavarian-influenced German variety.
